subject: Re: Fwd: NAPI monthly fee from LHW
body:
let geaorge decide

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

> Please advise what you wish for me to tell Peg.
>  
> Darren
> > Date: Mon, 31 Oct 2005 12:47:41 -0500
> To: dKIESQ@aol.com
> From: Peg Ugland <pegu@naproperty.com>
> Subject: NAPI monthly fee from LHW
> 
> Darren,
> Ohio has a new tax for commercial activity.  It is
> based on gross 
> receipts.  The tax is minimal this year for NAPI,
> but starting January 1, 
> 2006 the rate would be 1/4 of 1%.  Therefore, NAPI
> would have to pay the 
> tax on the $100,000 monthly fee from LHW.  Since LHW
> owns NAPI through the 
> WCTRII, couldn't we make these as capital
> contributions instead of a fee 
> which is taxed?
> Also, if the $100,000 is paid by LHW for Oct. & Nov.
> (paid 11/1/05 and 
> 12/1/05), NAPI will end up with about $450,000
> excess cash at 12/31/05 
> (rough estimate).  Do we want to forgo these last
> two payments in order not 
> to pay as much income tax?
> Thanks.
> Peg
> 
> 



	
		
__________________________________

body_html:
let geaorge decide

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

Please advise what you wish for me to tell Peg.

Darren

Date: Mon, 31 Oct 2005 12:47:41 -0500
To: dKIESQ@aol.com
From: Peg Ugland pegu@naproperty.com
Subject: NAPI monthly fee from LHW

Darren,
Ohio has a new tax for commercial activity. It is
based on gross
receipts. The tax is minimal this year for NAPI,
but starting January 1,
2006 the rate would be 1/4 of 1%. Therefore, NAPI
would have to pay the
tax on the $100,000 monthly fee from LHW. Since LHW
owns NAPI through the
WCTRII, couldn't we make these as capital
contributions instead of a fee
which is taxed?
Also, if the $100,000 is paid by LHW for Oct. & Nov.
(paid 11/1/05 and
12/1/05), NAPI will end up with about $450,000
excess cash at 12/31/05
(rough estimate). Do we want to forgo these last
two payments in order not
to pay as much income tax?
Thanks.
Peg

Yahoo! Mail - PC Magazine Editors' Choice 2005
http://mail.yahoo.com
